message.content: Look at this branch and https://github.com/moltis-org/moltis/pull/444 and fix any comments you see, solve them too.

3fff3c16 fix(browser): scope cached browser sessions per chat (#412)
55d5543c refactor(tools): split sandbox.rs into sandbox/ module directory

content.content:
{"body":"## Summary Some LLM providers (notably Kimi K2.5 via OpenRouter) mangle tool names when generating parallel tool calls: - Append numeric suffixes: `exec` → `exec_2`, `browser` → `browser_4` - Add `functions_` prefix + suffix: `spawn_agent` → `functions_spawn_agent_6` Moltis sends correct tool schemas, but the model returns mangled names. The current `sanitize_tool_name()` only handles whitespace/quotes, so these mangled names fail lookup with "unknown tool" errors. This PR enhances `sanitize_tool_name()` to strip these artifacts: - Strip `functions_` prefix (OpenAI legacy artifact from some models) - Strip trailing `_\d+` suffix when the suffix is purely numeric and the result is non-empty - Return `Cow<'_, str>` to avoid unnecessary allocations when no changes are needed - Add `debug!` tracing at both tool dispatch sites for visibility **No legitimate built-in or MCP tool name ends with `_\d+`** — verified via full audit of all 36+ registered tools and MCP naming convention (`mcp__server__tool`). ## Validation ### Completed - [x] `cargo test -p moltis-agents` — all 268 tests pass (14 sanitize_tool_name tests, including 5 new) - [x] `cargo +nightly-2025-11-30 fmt --all -- --check` — passes - [x] `cargo +nightly-2025-11-30 clippy -p moltis-agents --all-targets -- -D warnings` — passes clean - [x] No secrets or sensitive data in the diff - [x] Conventional commit message ### Remaining - [ ] `./scripts/local-validate.sh` (full CI-parity check — blocked by jemalloc build issue with spaces in iCloud path; the agents crate itself compiles and passes all checks) - [ ] Manual test with Kimi K2.5 via OpenRouter triggering parallel tool calls ## Manual QA 1. Configure an OpenRouter model that mangles parallel tool call names (e.g. Kimi K2.5) 2. Trigger a prompt that causes parallel tool calls 3. Verify tool calls succeed instead of failing with "unknown tool" errors 4. Check debug logs show "sanitized mangled tool name" entries ## Session Context This was implemented following a plan developed in the planning session. The plan is captured in `prompts/mellow-bubbling-candy.md`. Key decisions: - `Cow<'_, str>` return type avoids allocation on the common path (no mangling) - Suffix stripping requires `pos > 0` to avoid stripping a name that is purely `_\d+` - `functions_` prefix stripped before suffix to handle combined mangling (`functions_exec_2` → `exec`) - Tracing at dispatch sites (not inside the function) to include the tool call context","comments":[{"id":"IC_kwDOREW6tc7yXPx8","author":{"login":"greptile-apps"},"authorAssociation":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"

Greptile Summary

This PR enhances `sanitize_tool_name()` in `crates/agents/src/runner.rs` to handle tool-name mangling by certain LLM providers (notably Kimi K2.5 via OpenRouter), which append numeric parallel-call suffixes (`exec_2`) and/or prepend a `functions_` prefix (`functions_spawn_agent_6`). The fix returns `Cow<'_, str>` to avoid allocations on the common (no-mangling) path and adds `debug!` tracing at both tool dispatch sites. Key changes and observations: - The core suffix-stripping logic using `rfind` is correct for the single-suffix case and the `pos > 0` guard correctly prevents stripping `_2` to an empty string. - **Only the last `_\d+` suffix layer is stripped** — a doubly-mangled name like `exec_2_3` would resolve to `exec_2`, still failing lookup. No test covers this case. - **Hook dispatch receives the original unmangled name** (`tc.name`) rather than the sanitized value. Hook consumers checking `tool_name` in `BeforeToolCall`/`AfterToolCall` payloads would see the mangled name. - The `functions_` stripping is unconditional and load-bearing on the invariant that no current or future tool uses that prefix; this invariant is not enforced in code. - Test coverage for the new cases is thorough for the documented scenarios, including MCP names and real production tool names.

Confidence Score: 4/5

- Safe to merge for the targeted single-suffix mangling case; minor gaps in double-suffix handling and hook payload correctness. - The implementation is well-reasoned, cleanly written, and backed by 14 unit tests. The `Cow` return type is an appropriate optimization. The main concerns are: (1) only the last numeric suffix is stripped so `exec_2_3` → `exec_2` (not `exec`), which could silently fail; (2) hook dispatch forwards the original mangled name to hook consumers, which could cause policy mismatches; and (3) the `functions_` stripping relies on a non-enforced invariant about tool naming. None of these are blockers for the immediate fix, but they represent gaps worth addressing. - crates/agents/src/runner.rs — specifically the suffix-stripping loop and the `tc_name` value forwarded to hooks at both dispatch sites.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | crates/agents/src/runner.rs | Enhances `sanitize_tool_name` to return `Cow<'_, str>` and strip `functions_` prefix + trailing `_\d+` suffix from model-mangled parallel tool call names. Logic is correct for the documented cases, but only strips the last suffix layer (doubly-mangled names like `exec_2_3` → `exec_2` not `exec`), and hook dispatch sites continue to forward the original unmangled name to hook consumers. |

Flowchart

```mermaid %%{init: {'theme': 'neutral'}}%% flowchart TD A[sanitize_tool_name input] --> B[trim whitespace] B --> C{starts AND ends with quote?} C -- yes --> D[strip surrounding quotes] C -- no --> E[keep as-is] D --> F{starts with functions_ prefix?} E --> F F -- yes --> G[strip functions_ prefix] F -- no --> H[keep as-is] G --> I[rfind underscore in result] H --> I I --> J{suffix after last underscore is all digits AND pos is greater than 0?} J -- yes --> K[strip numeric suffix] J -- no --> L[keep as-is] K --> M{cleaned equals original name?} L --> M M -- yes --> N[return Cow::Borrowed - zero allocation] M -- no --> O[return Cow::Owned - allocates new string] N --> P[tools.get lookup] O --> P ``` Last reviewed commit: 2991e5b","createdAt":"2026-03-16T09:13:27Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/moltis-org/moltis/pull/444#issuecomment-4066180220","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOREW6tc7yYibI","author":{"login":"Temikus"},"authorAssociation":"NONE","body":"Addressed the review feedback in d5c5f9b5: - **`functions_` prefix invariant**: Added `// INVARIANT: no registered tool name starts with "functions_".` comment as suggested. - **Hook dispatch using mangled name**: Fixed both dispatch sites (non-streaming and streaming) to pass the sanitized tool name to hooks instead of the raw model output. This ensures hook policies like `block-dangerous-commands.sh` work correctly even when models mangle names. - **Double suffix stripping**: No change needed — single-pass is intentional to preserve legitimate names like `my_mcp_3000` (already resolved in thread).","createdAt":"2026-03-16T10:04:58Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/moltis-org/moltis/pull/444#issuecomment-4066518728","viewerDidAuthor":false}],"reviews":[{"id":"PRR_kwDOREW6tc7rlOua","author":{"login":"greptile-apps"},"authorAssociation":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"","submittedAt":"2026-03-16T09:13:34Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"reactionGroups":[],"state":"COMMENTED","commit":{"oid":"2991e5bc3452e8a5ab077f50148d5848d062e2fd"}},{"id":"PRR_kwDOREW6tc7rl31_","author":{"login":"Temikus"},"authorAssociation":"NONE","body":"","submittedAt":"2026-03-16T09:40:53Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"reactionGroups":[],"state":"COMMENTED","commit":{"oid":"2991e5bc3452e8a5ab077f50148d5848d062e2fd"}},{"id":"PRR_kwDOREW6tc7rl40-","author":{"login":"greptile-apps"},"authorAssociation":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"","submittedAt":"2026-03-16T09:41:23Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"reactionGroups":[],"state":"COMMENTED","commit":{"oid":"2991e5bc3452e8a5ab077f50148d5848d062e2fd"}},{"id":"PRR_kwDOREW6tc7rmcO6","author":{"login":"Temikus"},"authorAssociation":"NONE","body":"","submittedAt":"2026-03-16T10:03:25Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"reactionGroups":[],"state":"COMMENTED","commit":{"oid":"2991e5bc3452e8a5ab077f50148d5848d062e2fd"}},{"id":"PRR_kwDOREW6tc7rmcqo","author":{"login":"Temikus"},"authorAssociation":"NONE","body":"","submittedAt":"2026-03-16T10:03:38Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"reactionGroups":[],"state":"COMMENTED","commit":{"oid":"2991e5bc3452e8a5ab077f50148d5848d062e2fd"}}],"title":"fix(agents): sanitize model-mangled tool names from parallel calls"}

content.text: All three review comments appear to have been addressed in the latest commit (d5c5f9b5) based on the reply threads. Let me verify the actual code to confirm the fixes are properly in place.
Now let me check the two dispatch sites to verify hook dispatch uses the sanitized name.
Now let me check the invariant comment on the `functions_` prefix.

All three review comments are already resolved in the latest commit (d5c5f9b5):

1. **Double suffix stripping** - Temikus correctly declined this. Single-pass is intentional to preserve legitimate names like `my_mcp_3000`. Greptile agreed.

2. **`functions_` invariant comment** - Added at line 56: `// INVARIANT: no registered tool name starts with "functions_".`

3. **Hook dispatch using sanitized name** - Both dispatch sites (line 1057 and 1743) now derive `tc_name` from `sanitized.to_string()` instead of `tc.name.clone()`.

The PR is clean — all review feedback has been addressed. There's nothing left to fix.

message.content: Look at new comments from https://github.com/moltis-org/moltis/pull/444 fix and resolve conversations

input.command: gh api repos/moltis-org/moltis/pulls/444/comments --paginate | jq '[.[] | {id, author: .user.login, created_at, body, path, line, in_reply_to_id, diff_hunk}] | sort_by(.created_at) | .[-10:]'
gh api repos/moltis-org/moltis/pulls/444/comments --paginate | jq '[.[] | select(.in_reply_to_id == null)] | length'

content.text:
Two new comments from greptile (2026-03-23):

1. **Add invariant comment for suffix stripping** — matching the `functions_` invariant style
2. **Add test for `"functions_"` alone** — edge case yielding empty string

Let me fix both.

input.old_string:
// Strip trailing `_\d+` suffix (parallel-call indexing from some models).
    let cleaned = without_prefix

content.content: The file /Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/temikus/fix/sanitize-mangled-tool-names/crates/agents/src/runner.rs has been updated successfully.

lines: +        // "functions_" with no trailing name should produce an empty string,
+        // which is handled by find_empty_tool_name_call / EMPTY_TOOL_NAME_RETRY_PROMPT.

content.text: All 15 tests pass and formatting is clean. Let me commit and reply to the PR comments.

input.command:
git add crates/agents/src/runner.rs && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(agents): add suffix-stripping invariant comment and functions_ edge case test

- Add INVARIANT comment for the `_\d+` suffix stripping, matching the
  existing `functions_` prefix invariant style
- Add test for `"functions_"` alone yielding empty string (edge case
  handled by find_empty_tool_name_call)
EOF
)"
